page: Amazon Web Services c7gd.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by a custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical core operating at 2.6 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of system memory. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, providing low-latency local storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline speed of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reflect the single-core nature of this instance. Single-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, show average performance relative to the wider cloud market, while multi-core metrics are poor due to the single-vCPU limitation. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with read-only large block speeds at 30.49 GB/sec and write speeds at 10.89 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, achieve average performance tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7gd.medium offers a low-cost entry point into ARM-based compute-optimized hardware with integrated high-speed local storage. However, the resource density is low, making it less suitable for memory-intensive or highly parallelized workloads. The instance is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, development environments, and workloads requiring fast local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.medium is a compute-optimized instance featuring a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 core running at 2.6 GHz and 2.0 GB of system memory. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance includes a bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD instance store and a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark results indicate average single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain low due to the single-vCPU constraint. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, while database and Redis operations show lower throughput. The integrated NVMe storage provides high-speed local scratch space, making this instance a cost-effective option for lightweight, single-threaded applications. It is best suited for basic web serving, software development, and workloads requiring fast local temporary storage without heavy multi-threaded processing demands.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a single Graviton Neoverse-V1 core, 2.0 GB of RAM, and a 59 GB local NVMe SSD for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A single-core ARM64 cloud server featuring integrated local NVMe SSD storage and dedicated compute resources for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.

Bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD instance store for low-latency scratch space
2.0 GB of system memory and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications and basic web serving
